author: Annnnya
body:
#### PR description:

This pull request corresponds to ngt issue [#18](https://github.com/cms-ngt-hlt/ngt32/issues/18)

It changes the splitter to allow creating multiple-remote setups, where parameters for different processes are separated with ":"

It also changes the logic by which the **`MPIController`** and **`MPISource`** discover each other: instead of specifying ranks of the follower process, **`MPIController`** can set `remoteProcess` parameter with remote process name, and it's rank will be discovered at runtime. **`MPISource`** has to specify `thisProcessName` to get process name at initialization time. If there is only one controller and one follower process launched, these parameters are ignored.

Integration tests were modified to account for the configuration changes.

Issues found so far:

- if multiple controllers in the same local process expect remote with the same name, the program will hang instead of failing.
- if there are multiple remotes with the same name, the controller should proceed without failing even if they don't match the expected name (parameter was not passed)?


#### PR validation:

Tried using new splitter with the command:

```bash
edmMpiSplitConfig hlt.py -l local.py -m hltEcalDigisSoA hltEcalUncalibRecHitSoA -r remote_ecal.py -n ECAL :  \
-m hltHcalDigisSoA hltHbheRecoSoA hltParticleFlowRecHitHBHESoA hltParticleFlowClusterHBHESoA -d hltHcalDigis -r remote_hcal.py -n HCAL
```
title: Multiple controllers splitter
